violations.text: Establishment does not have an active individual with the Rhode Island Food Safety Manager license. Application emailed to PIC. Establishment to register. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2025

Molluscan Shellfish (steamers) located in the display case did not have the proper identification tags. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the Molluscan Shellstock are received until the container is empty. PIC obtained copy of tag from purveyor during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2026

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2026

Various foods in grab and go open air case were observed held at 46-56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Time temperature abused foods were voluntarily disposed of. Foods prepared today and recently were placed into walk-in freezer to blast chill. Foods were also observed stored beyond load limit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2026

Windex was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items (in main kitchen and meatcutting area). Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2026

Various foods made in the food establishment are lacking labels and/or missing information. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients (including sub ingredients), the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2026
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2026

The following were observed in poor repair: 1. bottom of robot coupe 2. meat room cutting board 3. large walk-in cooler door and gaskets 4. Recycling bin lids (cracked) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/24/2024

Open air case was observed at ranging ambient temperatures of 41-46 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Fan was observed unplugged. Unit to be monitored and repaired if needed. No foods to be stored in unit until properly adjusted and can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2026
Bandsaw lid was observed held together by zipties. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2026
Establishment does not have an active individual with the Rhode Island Food Safety Manager license. Application emailed to PIC. Establishment to register. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2025
Multiple hand sinks are lacking notification signs. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/24/2024
Working containers of chemicals unlabeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024

Grab n Go foods packaged for self service sale is lacking all required information. Food labels must include common name of food, ingredient list (including sub ingredients), name of business, business address and content's weight or value. New Violation. Correct By: 12/24/2024
Food observed on the floor in freezer. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024

The following were observed in poor repair: 1. bottom of robot coupe 2. produce cutting board and meat room cutting board 3. large walk-in cooler door and gaskets 4. Recycling bin lids (cracked) New Violation. Correct By: 12/24/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the speedracks in the exterior walk-in cooler and various exteriors of equipment have an accumulation of soil residue. New Violation. Correct By: 12/24/2024

The following areas have an accumulation of soil residue: 1. floors under equipment in kitchen 2. floors in the walk-in coolers Physical facilities to be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/24/2024
